title: Vicia mulleriana
intro: Vicia mulleriana, also known as Müller's vetch or Müller's pea, is a perennial legume plant native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Vicia mulleriana include alkaloids, flavonoids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Vicia mulleriana is often prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture, and is commonly used in the form of teas, syrups, and cough medicines.
taxonomy: Vicia mulleriana is a species of flowering plant in the pea family, Fabaceae. It is a legume native to China. Within the Fabaceae family, Vicia mulleriana belongs to the genus Vicia. Vicia mulleriana is classified as a type of vetch, which is a common name for plants within the genus Vicia. The exact classification of Vicia mulleriana is Vicia mulleriana (Franch.) Schindl., but it may also be referred to as Vicia mulleriana (Franch.) Schindl. ex Pamp..
uses: Vicia mulleriana has been reported to have medicinal properties, particularly in traditional Chinese medicine.
